title: Carex lazarei
taxonomy: Carex lazarei is classified as a species within the genus Carex, which is a group of perennial plants commonly known as sedges. This species belongs to the family Cyperaceae, which includes various types of sedges and rushes. Carex lazarei is further categorized as a member of the order Poales, a group of monocot flowering plants that also includes grasses and bromeliads. At the kingdom level, Carex lazarei is classified as Plantae, which includes all types of plants. Further classification includes the division Magnoliophyta, the phylum Magnoliophyta, and the rank class Liliopsida.

intro: Carex lazarei, also known as Lazare's sedge, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Cyperaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems. The bioactive constituents of Carex lazarei include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Lazare's sedge is typically used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat digestive issues and respiratory problems.
